---
title: "nitrix.stats.gam"
generated: true
signature-hash: e947076f6a900be4
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub ‚Äî fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.gam`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC ¬ß6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map ‚Äî voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `N` | observations ‚Äî time points, subjects, or sampled points |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `GAMResult` {#GAMResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GAMResult -->
```python
class GAMResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:GAMResult -->
Per-element GAM fit output.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GAMResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(coef: Float[Array, 'V p'], lam: Float[Array, 'V m'], edf: Float[Array, 'V m'], edf_total: Float[Array, 'V'], dispersion: Float[Array, 'V'], deviance: Float[Array, 'V'], null_deviance: Float[Array, 'V'], cov_unscaled: Float[Array, 'V p p'], family: Family, n_obs: int, col_slices: Tuple[Tuple[int, int], ...], intercept: bool) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `SmoothTest` {#SmoothTest}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SmoothTest -->
```python
class SmoothTest(NamedTuple)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:SmoothTest -->
Per-smooth approximate-significance test (Wood 2013, integer-rank).

Each field is `(V, m)` -- one column per smooth (in `smooths` order), one
row per element.  `stat` is the test statistic `T_r`, `rank` its
reference degrees of freedom, `p_value` the upper-tail p (chi-square for a
fixed-dispersion family, `F` with `N - edf_total` denominator df
otherwise), and `edf` the smooth's effective degrees of freedom (the
`mgcv::summary.gam` "edf" column).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gam_fit` {#gam_fit}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gam_fit -->
```python
gam_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], smooths: Sequence[Smooth], *, parametric: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None, intercept: bool = True, family: Union[str, Family] = GAUSSIAN, lambda_mode: Literal['per_element', 'shared'] = 'per_element', n_outer: int = 20, n_inner: int = 10, ridge: float = 1e-08, lam_floor: float = 1e-06, lam_ceil: float = 100000000.0, block: Optional[int] = None) -> GAMResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gam_fit -->
Fit a mass-univariate GAM: shared smooth bases, per-element responses.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gam_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` √ó `N` | ‚Äî | `(V, N)` responses. |
| `smooths` | `Sequence[Smooth]` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Penalised smooth bases (one per smooth term): a `SplineBasis` (`bspline_basis` / `thinplate_regression_basis` / `cyclic_cubic_basis`) or a `TensorBasis` (`tensor_product_basis`) for an anisotropic interaction. A tensor smooth carries one smoothing parameter per margin, all selected by the same Fellner-Schall loop. |
| `parametric` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Optional `(N, q)` unpenalised linear design (covariates entering linearly). The intercept is added separately (see `intercept`). |
| `intercept` | `bool` | ‚Äî | `True` | Prepend an intercept column (default `True`). |
| `family` | `Union[str, Family]` | ‚Äî | `GAUSSIAN` | Exponential family (default `GAUSSIAN`). |
| `lambda_mode` | `Literal['per_element', 'shared']` | ‚Äî | `'per_element'` | `'per_element'` (default) selects a smoothing parameter per element (ModelArray parity). `'shared'` selects **one** smoothing parameter across all elements via a pooled Fellner-Schall update on sufficient statistics -- an `O(n_outer p^3)`, `V`-independent outer loop (much faster when smoothness is homogeneous across the brain). Gaussian only. |
| `n_outer` | `int` | ‚Äî | `20` | Fellner-Schall outer iterations and penalised-IRLS inner iterations. |
| `n_inner` | `int` | ‚Äî | `10` | Fellner-Schall outer iterations and penalised-IRLS inner iterations. |
| `ridge` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-08` | Small stabiliser on the penalised normal equations. |
| `lam_floor` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-06` | Clamp on each smoothing parameter. |
| `lam_ceil` | `float` | ‚Äî | `100000000.0` | Clamp on each smoothing parameter. |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Optional element-block size bounding peak memory (the per-element `(V, p, p)` covariances) on brain-scale `V`. `None` (default) is a single `vmap`. |

**Returns** `GAMResult`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gam_predict` {#gam_predict}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gam_predict -->
```python
gam_predict(result: GAMResult, smooths: Sequence[Smooth], x_smooths: Sequence[Any], *, parametric: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None, type: Literal['response', 'link'] = 'response') -> Float[Array, 'V N']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gam_predict -->
Per-element GAM prediction on (new) covariates.

Reassembles the fitted design `[intercept | parametric | B_1(x_1) | ...]`
at the new covariates -- evaluating each smooth's basis at `x_smooths[k]`
with the **same** fitted knots/penalty (`smooth.eval_design`, the
machinery :func:`smooth_partial_effect` uses for one block) and the stored
`result.col_slices` / `result.intercept` layout -- then returns
`eta = design @ coef` (`type='link'`) or `family.linkinv(eta)`
(`type='response'`, default).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gam_predict -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GAMResult` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | A :class:`GAMResult` from :func:`gam_fit`. |
| `smooths` | `Sequence[Smooth]` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | The same `Smooth` terms passed to :func:`gam_fit`, in the same order (used here only to evaluate the basis at the new covariates). |
| `x_smooths` | `Sequence[Any]` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | New covariate(s) per smooth (one entry per `smooths` term, each in the form that `smooth.eval_design` accepts -- an `(N,)` grid, a tuple of per-margin grids for a tensor smooth, or integer level indices for a random-effect basis). All terms must evaluate to the same row count `N`. |
| `parametric` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(N, q)` parametric design at the new covariates; `None` if the fit had none. Must match the fit's parametric column count. |
| `type` | `Literal['response', 'link']` | ‚Äî | `'response'` | `'response'` (the mean, via the link inverse) or `'link'` (the linear predictor `eta`). |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `V` √ó `N`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `smooth_partial_effect` {#smooth_partial_effect}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:smooth_partial_effect -->
```python
smooth_partial_effect(result: GAMResult, smooth_index: int, basis: Smooth, x: Union[Float[Array, ' g'], Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]]) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:smooth_partial_effect -->
Per-element partial effect of one smooth on a covariate grid `x`.

Returns `(effect, se)`: the fitted smooth `B(x) gamma_k` and its
pointwise standard error from the Bayesian covariance block (for a
credible band).  `basis` is the smooth used to build the term; for a
`TensorBasis` pass `x` as a tuple of matched per-margin grids (all
length `g`) and the effect is the interaction surface along that path.
For a `REBasis` pass `x` as the integer level indices to read off -- the
effect is then the per-level random effect (the BLUP intercept, or the
random-slope coefficient) at those levels.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:smooth_partial_effect -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GAMResult` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `smooth_index` | `int` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `basis` | `Smooth` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `x` | `Union[Float[Array, ' g'], Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]]` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `smooth_significance` {#smooth_significance}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:smooth_significance -->
```python
smooth_significance(result: GAMResult, smooths: Sequence[Smooth]) -> SmoothTest
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:smooth_significance -->
Approximate significance test for each smooth term (Wood 2013).

The `mgcv::summary.gam` smooth-term test in its integer-rank form
(`mgcv:::testStat` with `type=1`): project the smooth's coefficients
through its design's QR (`R`, `R^T R = X^T X`), eigendecompose the
projected Bayesian covariance `R V R^T`, and form the rank-truncated
quadratic form `T_r = beta^T V_r^- beta` with the rank set to the rounded
effective df.  `T_r` is approximately `chi^2_r` for a fixed-dispersion
family, or `F_{r, N - edf_total}` for an estimated scale -- the single
"is `s(x)` significant, edf, p" line a developmental / brain-age GAM
analyst reads off `summary.gam`.

`smooths` is the same sequence passed to :func:`gam_fit` (their `.design`
supplies each term's model matrix).  The test uses the forward-only
`sym_eig_jacobi` and is **not** differentiated through.

Returns a :class:`SmoothTest` of `(V, m)` arrays.  The fractional-rank
refinement (the `mgcv` default `type=0`, which needs the weighted-
chi-square CDF) is a documented follow-up; the integer-rank test reproduces
`testStat(..., type=1)` and tracks the default closely.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:smooth_significance -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GAMResult` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `smooths` | `Sequence[Smooth]` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `SmoothTest`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
